name: 1947 Kelani River Basin Floods
description: One of the most devastating floods in recorded Sri Lankan history. The Kelani river overflowed its banks causing massive destruction across the western province, permanently altering infrastructure planning around Colombo.

name: 2003 Southern Monsoon Floods & Landslides
description: Triggered by exceptionally heavy rainfall, the Gin, Kalu, and Nilwala rivers burst, creating severe flooding and triggering massive landslides in Ratnapura.

name: 2010 Western Province Flash Floods
description: Rapid localized downpours caused severe flash flooding in the Colombo metropolitan area, paralyzing the city and submerging the parliament premises.

name: 2011 Eastern Coast Deluge
description: An unprecedented Northeast Monsoon depression dropped more than 1000mm of rain. Approximately 300,000 people were displaced, and major agricultural lands were destroyed.

name: 2011 Tropical Storm Thane Impact
description: Severe weather conditions driven by Cyclone Thane brought localized flooding and strong winds to the Northern province.

name: 2012 End-of-Year Monsoons
description: Weeks of continuous heavy rainfall forced authorities to open major reservoir spillways, flooding low-lying areas in the North Central province.

name: 2014 Kelani Basin Floods
description: Strong Southwest Monsoon winds and extreme rain caused the Kelani and Kalu rivers to overflow, leading to widespread power outages and road blockages.

name: 2016 Tropical Cyclone Roanu
description: Brought extreme rains that caused the deadly Aranayake landslide in Kegalle and catastrophic flooding along the Kelani River that submerged major parts of Colombo.

name: 2017 Southwest Monsoon Catastrophe
description: The worst flooding since 2003, triggered by anomalous monsoon rains. Rapid rising of the Kalu, Nilwala, and Gin rivers devastated the southern half of the country.

name: 2018 Puththalam & Western Floods
description: An active Southwest Monsoon brought massive deluges over the western coast, heavily impacting Puttalam and causing severe urban flooding in Gampaha.

name: 2019 Post-Monsoon Coastal Floods
description: Late-season monsoon squalls hit the southern coastal belt, resulting in localized flash floods, minor property damage, and localized evacuations.

name: 2021 Cyclone Yaas Aftermath
description: The tail-end effects of Cyclone Yaas in the Bay of Bengal intensified the monsoon, causing the Kalu River and Kelani River to breach minor flood levels.

name: 2021 Northeast Monsoon Deluge
description: An intense depression system triggered widespread flooding in the Northern and North-Western provinces, destroying thousands of acres of paddy fields.

name: 2022 Central Highlands Landslides & Floods
description: Unseasonal rains triggered flash floods and landslides in the central hill country, destroying estate line rooms and blocking major transport routes.

name: 2023 October Monsoon Floods
description: A very intense onset of the monsoon submerged major parts of Matara and Galle districts. The Nilwala river remained at major flood levels for over a week.

name: 2023 Eastern Coast Depression
description: A late-year atmospheric depression brought huge rainfall to the eastern slopes. Spilling irrigation tanks caused downstream flooding in Polonnaruwa.
